questions.question: Which CSS property is critical for inputs to include padding in the total width?
questions.explanation: box-sizing: border-box makes padding and border included in the element's total width, preventing input overflow issues.

questions.question: Which pseudo-class styles an input with invalid data?
questions.explanation: :invalid selects form elements whose content fails validation against the input constraints defined by HTML attributes.

questions.question: What should you NOT remove without providing a replacement when styling form elements?
questions.explanation: Never remove outline: none without providing a visible focus indicator. Focus outlines are essential for keyboard accessibility.

questions.question: Which property enables custom checkbox styling?
questions.explanation: appearance: none removes the native OS styling from form controls, giving you a blank canvas for custom checkbox styling.

questions.question: What layout method is recommended for form structure?
questions.explanation: CSS Grid and Flexbox provide the most responsive and maintainable form layouts, with consistent spacing via the gap property.

questions.question: What is the minimum touch target size recommended for form controls on mobile?
questions.explanation: WCAG recommends touch targets of at least 44x44px for comfortable and accurate tapping on mobile devices.

questions.question: Which pseudo-class displays validation errors only after user interaction?
questions.explanation: :user-invalid only applies after the user has interacted with the field, preventing premature error display on empty required fields.

questions.question: What CSS property should be used to inherit typography from the design system onto form controls?
questions.explanation: Form controls don't inherit font properties automatically. Setting font-family: inherit and font-size: inherit ensures they match the design system.

questions.question: How do you style a custom select dropdown arrow without JavaScript?
options: Use appearance: none with a background-image SVG arrow
questions.explanation: Setting appearance: none removes the native arrow, then a custom SVG arrow is applied as a background-image on the select element.

questions.question: Which pseudo-class highlights the entire form group when any child input is focused?
questions.explanation: :focus-within on the parent container applies styles when any descendant element has focus, useful for highlighting active form sections.

questions.question: What is the accessibility requirement for associating labels with inputs?
options: Label must be visually near the input
Every input must have an associated label using for/id or wrapping
aria-label is required on all inputs
questions.explanation: Every input needs a proper label element associated via the for/id attributes or by wrapping the input in the label element.

questions.question: Why is using placeholder as a label replacement problematic?
options: They are not accessible as labels
questions.explanation: Placeholders disappear on input, often have low contrast, and are not a replacement for proper labels per WCAG guidelines.

questions.question: Which pseudo-class is specifically for keyboard-only focus indicators?
questions.explanation: :focus-visible shows a focus indicator only when the browser determines it's keyboard-initiated, not on mouse clicks.

questions.question: What is the easiest way to theme native checkboxes and radio buttons in modern CSS?
options: Use appearance: none with custom styles
questions.explanation: accent-color provides quick native theming for checkboxes, radio buttons, and range inputs without custom CSS or JavaScript.

questions.question: Which pseudo-class styles an input that is showing its placeholder text?
questions.explanation: :placeholder-shown applies when the input's placeholder text is visible (no user input yet), useful for float label patterns.

questions.question: What is the correct approach for styling custom radio buttons?
options: Use appearance: none, set dimensions, use ::after for the dot
questions.explanation: appearance: none removes native styling, then custom dimensions, border, and a ::after pseudo-element create the custom radio indicator.

questions.question: How should error messages be programmatically associated with inputs?
options: Place error message next to the input
Use aria-describedby on the input pointing to the error element
questions.explanation: aria-describedby or aria-errormessage associates the error message with the input, so screen readers announce it when the input receives focus.

questions.question: What CSS selector styles the track of a range input?
options: Both webkit and mozz prefixed pseudo-elements
questions.explanation: Range input styling requires vendor-prefixed pseudo-elements like ::-webkit-slider-runnable-track and ::-moz-range-track.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the inputmode attribute in form design?
options: Triggers the appropriate mobile keyboard layout
questions.explanation: inputmode tells mobile browsers which keyboard layout to show (numeric, email, url, etc.), improving the mobile input experience.

questions.question: Which HTML elements should be used to group related radio buttons for accessibility?
questions.explanation: <fieldset> groups related form controls and <legend> provides the group label, which screen readers announce when navigating between options.
